findings.title: Conservativeness proof holds in V but the N choice (n=max_runs) breaks the upper-bound; only masked by the frozen edge=1.0 being unconfirmable
findings.code_ref: src/backend/backtesting/gates/power_canary.py:45 (n = max(search_size or n_trials, n_trials, 2)); run.py:61 (n_trials=max(max_runs,2)); module docstring lines 8-16
findings.detail: The module claims 'power monotone-decreasing in V, so power_at_floor < FLOOR ⟹ real power < FLOOR — no dependence on the run's measured dispersion.' I verified V-monotonicity numerically (t=1000,N=50: V=1e-4→0.415, 3e-4→0.240, 1e-3→0.020, ≥3e-3→0.000) and the exposure/T directions are conservative (sparse exposure raises the real floor V; calendar t_bars over-estimates real T after warm-up → both lower real power). BUT the proof is silent on N, and N goes the WRONG way. Power is DECREASING in N (t=2268: N=2→0.660, N=5→0.265, N=20→0.010, N=100→0.000). The canary evaluates at N=max_runs (default 100), while the real gate's firm regime uses n_search=max(search_size,n_trials_global), and a single run without coverage_dsr can execute as few as the firm minimum (PROVISIONAL_BELOW=20) up to max_runs. Since power decreases in N and N_canary=max_runs ≥ N_real, canary_power ≤ real_power in the N dimension — i.e. the canary is a LOWER bound, not an upper bound, on real firm power. So 'canary vacuous' does NOT logically imply 'real gate vacuous'; the real gate (fewer executed trials → smaller N → more power) could still confirm the edge. This is currently harmless ONLY because at the frozen reference edge (1.0 annualized) the max real firm power (at N=20) is ≤0.010 for every realistic T — I scanned t∈[30,3000] against N∈{20,50,100,200,500,1000} and the vacuity verdict never flips. The mechanism is therefore sound today by accident of the weak edge, not by the stated V-argument. Fix (strictly-more-conservative, behavior-neutral today): evaluate the canary at the firm-minimum N=20 (the smallest gate-evaluable N, hence MAX real firm power), so canary_power becomes a true upper bound and the conservativeness claim is rigorous rather than incidental. This matters the moment CANARY_EDGE or the T regime is ever retuned.

findings.title: PF4 null-variance floor is reimplemented in miniature inside the canary; freeze/tamper test only pins scalars, not the formula → silent drift risk
findings.code_ref: power_canary.py:43-45 (v = V_NULL_INFLATION/max(t_bars-1,1); n = max(...)); deflated_sharpe.py:137-157 (real floor); test_gate_config_freeze.py:56-63
findings.detail: The canary passes synthetic streams through deflated_sharpe() directly and re-derives the floor inline as v=V_NULL_INFLATION/(t_bars-1) with n=max(search_size or n_trials, n_trials, 2). The real DeflatedSharpeGate.check floor is richer: v_null=V_NULL_INFLATION*max(1/n_eff_cand, 1/(t_trials-1)) where n_eff_cand=exposure*(len-1), plus n_search=max(supplied_search_size, n_trials). For the dense case (exposure=1, no trial_median_t) the two coincide exactly (both = 3/(len-1)), so there is no current numeric mismatch — good. But V_NULL_INFLATION and THRESHOLD are the ONLY shared symbols (imported from the gate); the floor STRUCTURE is duplicated. The new freeze test test_mon1_canary_constants_match_the_freeze pins only EDGE/HEALTH_EDGE/REPS/SEED/POWER_FLOOR — it does not assert the canary's floor formula tracks the gate's. If the gate's floor is later restructured (e.g. a different inv_null, an added clock, or the n_search valve changes), the canary will silently keep the old formula and its conservativeness argument breaks with no test failure. Recommend factoring the null-variance floor into a shared DeflatedSharpeGate classmethod (e.g. null_variance_floor(t_cand, exposure, t_trials)) that both the gate and the canary call. Note: wholesale calling gate.check with a synthetic ctx is NOT the right fix — the gate returns provisional PASS for n<20, which would corrupt the raw pass-rate the canary needs (esp. the N=2 health probe); the canary legitimately needs raw deflated_sharpe, only the floor helper should be shared.

findings.title: Health self-check false-negatives at small T and validates the wrong N path
findings.detail: health = power of the absurd 6.0 edge at N=2. Numerically health(t=30)=0.280, health(t=50)=0.450 — both below CANARY_POWER_FLOOR=0.5, so canary_healthy=False and _resolve_soft_dsr RAISES ('the MON1 canary self-check failed — no honest vacuity claim is possible'). The direction is SAFE (a refusal blocks softening), but two issues: (1) the health check conflates 'measurement mechanism broken' with 'T simply too small for even a 6.0 edge to clear the floor hurdle' — for any window near the 45-day floor (t_bars≈31, health≈0.28) soft_dsr is unconditionally refused with a message implying the canary is broken, which is misleading. (2) The health probe fixes N=2, but the actual vacuity claim runs at N=max_runs; a fault in the large-N sr0/expected-max path would not be caught by an N=2 probe. Consider probing health at the same N used for the reference claim, and/or distinguishing 'measurement broken' from 'operating point too small to adjudicate'.

findings.title: MC noise (reps=200) leaves the small-T health verdict within one standard error of the 0.5 floor
findings.detail: A proportion over 200 reps has se≈sqrt(p(1-p)/200)=0.035 at p=0.5. health(t=50)=0.450 sits ~1.4 se below 0.5; the frozen seed makes the verdict deterministic but the TRUE value is within MC error of the floor, so the healthy/refuse boundary for short windows is essentially a coin-flip decided by the seed. No unsound outcome results (both sides only toggle a safe refusal vs an activation that is itself justified because edge=1.0 is genuinely unconfirmable at t=50), so severity is low — but if the health floor is meant to be a hard gate, either raise reps for the health probe or add a margin (e.g. health ≥ 0.5 + 2·se) so the deterministic verdict is not sensitive to a single seed near the boundary.

findings.title: RT2 activation guard is near-tautological: the canary reports vacuous for essentially every realistic robustness run
findings.detail: At the PF4 floor V=3/(T-1) with N≥20, the expected-max-of-junk hurdle exceeds the 1.0 reference edge (at the default window t_bars=2268, N=100 the annualized sr0≈1.46 > 1.0), so power_at_reference=0.0 and vacuous=True for the default run and for all realistic (T,N). The guard only ever refuses on vacuity grounds at non-physical operating points (the MON1 test forces this with t_bars=50000 ≈ 200 years) or when the small-T health check trips. Net effect: the much-emphasized RT2 third precondition ('canary-proven vacuity') is, in practice, always satisfied whenever soft_dsr is requested in robustness+OOS mode — the canary functions as evidence/telemetry, not as a discriminating gate. This is internally consistent (the PF4 floor deliberately makes a 1.0-Sharpe edge unconfirmable), but the owner should know the activation condition is effectively 'on when requested' rather than a meaningful additional filter, and decide whether CANARY_EDGE (1.0) vs the actual floor hurdle (~1.46 annualized) is the intended reference band.

result.summary: Canary-soundness review of MON1/RT2 (Track 6). The core conservativeness claim is CORRECT in the V dimension — I numerically confirmed power is monotone-decreasing in the null variance, and the exposure-aware sparse floor plus the calendar over-estimate of T both push the real floor V ABOVE the canary's dense floor, so real power ≤ canary power along V/T (every mismatch resolves to a safe/conservative refusal). No blocker. The one genuine soundness gap (HIGH, latent): the proof is silent on N, and N goes the wrong way — the canary evaluates at n=max_runs while the real firm gate can run at fewer trials (down to the firm minimum 20), and since power DECREASES in N, canary power is a LOWER bound (not an upper bound) on real firm power there. 'Canary vacuous' therefore does not strictly imply 'real gate vacuous'; it is saved today only because the frozen edge=1.0 has ≤0.010 power at every N≥20 for realistic T (I scanned t∈[30,3000] — the vacuity verdict never flips between N=20 and larger N). Recommend evaluating at the firm-minimum N=20 (max real firm power) to make the bound rigorous rather than incidental — a behavior-neutral hardening. Secondary: the PF4 floor formula is duplicated inline in the canary (matches the gate for the dense case now, but the freeze test pins only scalar constants, not the formula → drift risk; share a floor helper); the N=2 health probe false-negatives at small T (safe direction but conflates 'broken' with 'T too small' and validates the wrong N path); reps=200 leaves the small-T health verdict within ~1 se of the 0.5 floor; and RT2's 'canary-proven vacuity' precondition is effectively always true for real runs (telemetry, not a discriminating gate). D2 excess_sharpe wiring is NOT dead (populated in loop.py/state.py). Verified with the project venv, PYTHONPATH=., BROKER_MODE=mock.

findings.title: UI pins oos_min_sharpe=0.9 explicitly, defeating the frozen None-defer single-source contract
findings.detail: new/page.tsx initializes oosMinSharpe=useState(0.9) and the payload sends `oos_min_sharpe: oosMinSharpe` on EVERY run (line ~204), never null — including runs where the user never touched the slider. This directly contradicts the design invariant codified in tests/unit/backtesting/gates/test_gate_config_freeze.py::test_api_and_run_defaults_defer_to_the_single_frozen_constant ("the 0.9 default lives in exactly ONE place (loop.py); the API and run layer pass None -> resolved to the frozen constant, so the tamper-wire covers the whole chain"). The frozen quality_floor_default=0.9 (coverage-v2-gate-config.json line 15) is enforced against loop.OOS_MIN_SHARPE_DEFAULT by the tamper test, but the frontend now duplicates that constant OUTSIDE the tamper wire. Functionally identical today (0.9==0.9), but it is exactly the freeze-drift the tamper mechanism exists to prevent: if the frozen default is ever re-frozen to another value, every UI-started run silently keeps pinning 0.9 while CLI/None-default runs move — the whole-chain coverage the test asserts is broken at the one link the user actually uses. Adjudication: this IS the drift risk. Fix: send null when the slider sits at its default / was untouched (or omit the key), preserving None-defer; only send an explicit value when the user deliberately moves it.

findings.title: D2 excess_sharpe / excess_total_return_net are never persisted — chip is live-memory-only, silently vanishes after restart for ALL runs
findings.detail: The read path added at router.py:891-893 and :953-954 pulls oos_excess_sharpe/oos_excess_total_return_net, but only the LIVE in-memory branch (_candidate_detail_or_404, reading rec.state.oos_results via getattr(o,...)) ever supplies them. The DB branch persistence.load_candidate_detail (persistence.py:417-431) returns a fixed dict with NO excess keys, and persist_snapshot (persistence.py:204) writes only oos_outcome; ResearchCandidateDB (db_models.py:94) has no excess columns at all. Consequence: the null-handling claimed in task item (d) is correct but broader than framed — the chip is hidden not just for OLD runs but for EVERY run once its RunRecord leaves _runs (process restart / server redeploy, which the OSS deploy flow does routinely). The buy-and-hold reality-check evidence is non-durable: it disappears from completed runs after a restart even though oos_outcome (the PASS it gates) survives. Graceful (no crash), but a displayed validation metric is lost. Fix: persist excess_sharpe/excess_total_return_net onto ResearchCandidateDB and return them from load_candidate_detail so the DB path matches the live path.

findings.title: Canary vacuity proof assumes candidate bar-count <= window-derived t_bars; airtight only for daily bars on the fixed robustness window
findings.detail: gate_vacuity_canary evaluates at v = V_NULL/(t_bars-1) with t_bars = window_days*252/365 and exposure implicitly 1.0. The monotone-in-V vacuity argument requires canary_v <= real_v_used, i.e. exposure*t_cand <= t_bars-1. This holds with margin for the current regime: soft_dsr is robustness-only, robustness always uses the fixed strategist window (WINDOW_START/END, daily bars, t_bars=2268 verified), and real exposure<=1 plus the trial-clock only raise real V (more conservative). But the guarantee is not structural: if a future candidate ran materially more bars than the window estimate (e.g. intraday returns) with near-full exposure, real_v could fall below the canary floor and the 'vacuous at floor => real vacuity' implication would no longer hold. Currently inert; document the daily-bar/fixed-window assumption alongside the freeze constants so it cannot silently regress if research adds intraday support.

result.summary: Reviewed the Track 6 uncommitted diff (MON1 power_canary.py, RT2 activation in run.py, MON2 deflated_sharpe details, D2/MON3 frontend, freeze+tamper). WIRING/CONTRACT is largely sound: (a) on_event(kind,payload) signatures match end-to-end and raw_kind is mapped verbatim so the MON3 banner reads the pre-loop mon1_canary event; (b) a canary ValueError is surfaced loudly as run status=failed with message (on_start runs before the canary, so state.error_message is set); the MON1 vacuity claim is numerically verified (power_at_reference=0.01<<0.5 on the fixed robustness window) and structurally conservative in V, N, exposure and trial-clock; coverage_dsr dependent-checkbox, soft_dsr false-in-regime, and MON2 z=1.645/THRESHOLD=0.95 are all consistent; freeze+tamper tests pass 18/18. Two issues to fix before commit: HIGH — the new UI always pins oos_min_sharpe=0.9 explicitly, duplicating the frozen constant outside the tamper wire and defeating the None-defer single-source contract that test_api_and_run_defaults_defer_to_the_single_frozen_constant explicitly guards (latent freeze-drift). MEDIUM — the D2 excess_sharpe/excess_total_return_net are computed and read but never persisted (no DB columns, load_candidate_detail omits them), so the buy-and-hold chip is live-memory-only and silently disappears for all runs after any server restart, not just old runs. Plus one LOW documentation note on the canary's bar-count assumption. No blocker: the vacuity claim is sound and no contract is broken.
